commit.message:
fix: Set embedding_model default to match current .env (Qwen3-Embedding-8B)

The embedding_model default was hardcoded to BAAI/bge-m3 (1024-dim),
but .env uses Qwen/Qwen3-Embedding-8B (4096-dim), causing a dimension
mismatch when ChromaDB collection was initialized.

Now the default matches the actual .env configuration, ensuring
consistency between the model used for embedding and the collection
dimension.

commit.message:
fix: restore `override` lock-file handling after launcher refactor (#4532)

* fix: update test to assert warnings for invalid CLI arguments

* chore: adding changelog file 4519.fixed.md [dependabot-skip]

* feat: add log printing step to test-remote workflow for better debugging

* fix: clear MAPDL database before resume() to prevent segfault in LoadElements

MAPDL was crashing with a Segmentation Violation in LoadElements during
CI. The crash was triggered by the _save_selection context manager
(which creates temporary __CMP_*__ components and runs NSLE/LoadElements)
encountering a stale or inconsistent database state left over from a
previous test.

Root cause: function-scoped resume() fixtures in test_post.py, and
class-scoped setup fixtures in test_component.py and
test_component_queries.py, were loading or creating database state
without first clearing any leftover state from prior tests.

Changes:
- tests/test_post.py: add clear(mapdl) before mapdl.resume() in all
  four resume fixtures (Test_static_solve, Test_plastic_solve,
  Test_contact_solve, Test_thermal_solve); remove redundant prep7()/
  solution() calls superseded by clear(); make Test_thermal_solve
  inherit TestClass for consistent class-level cleanup; import clear
  from conftest
- tests/test_component.py: add clear(mapdl) at the start of
  Test_components.setup to make the cleanup explicit and remove
  the implicit ordering dependency on TestClass.initializer; import
  clear from conftest
- tests/test_inline_functions/test_component_queries.py: add
  clear(mapdl) at the start of solved_box and twisted_sheet class-
  scoped fixtures; import clear from conftest
- docker/testing/Dockerfile: enable PYMAPDL_DEBUG_TESTING for better
  CI diagnostics
- docker/testing/docker-compose.yml: comment out custom entrypoint/
  command to restore default MAPDL startup behaviour

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Co-authored-by: Copilot <175728472+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: unused import

* fix: restore override lock-file handling after launcher refactor

The check_lock_file() logic was not ported when launcher.py was split
into modules in PR #4416.  As a result, override=True had no effect and
MAPDL would fail to start whenever a stale .lock file existed in the
run_location directory.

- validation.py: add _validate_lock_file(); raises an error when a lock
  file is present and override=False, or a warning when override=True
  (file will be removed before launch).  Called from validate_config().
- process.py: add _handle_lock_file(); deletes the lock file when
  override=True, raises LockFileException when override=False (safety
  net in case validation was bypassed).  Called at the top of
  launch_mapdl_process().
- tests: 5 new tests in TestValidateLockFile, 6 in TestHandleLockFile.

Fixes #4526

* fix: skip validation for HPC launches when lock file is on remote cluster

commit.message:
feat(ws): structure-aware repo matching, red errors, --all flag exclusivity

Three targeting improvements:

1. Structure-aware --repo-name matching
   For 'list' structure workspaces, --repo-name now accepts the last
   slash-delimited segment of a repo's config name (with .git stripped),
   so '-r api' matches 'org/team/api' and 'org/team/api.git'.
   Exact full-name match is still accepted for both structures.
   - internal/workspace/targeting.go: new repoNameMatches() helper
   - internal/workspace/exec.go: all 3 filter checks use repoNameMatches
   - internal/workspace/workspace.go: all 6 filter checks use repoNameMatches
   - internal/workspace/targeting_test.go: 4 new list-structure tests

2. Red error messages
   All errors returned from subcommand RunE are now printed in red to
   stderr. Previously cobra printed them as plain 'Error: <msg>' text.
   - zgard/root.go: SilenceErrors/SilenceUsage set to true; Execute()
     prints err via clr.Red to stderr before os.Exit(1)

3. --all mutual exclusivity with --project-name / --repo-name
   Using --all together with -p/--project-name or -r/--repo-name is
   now a hard error — the flags are logically incompatible since --all
   operates across every workspace, while project/repo filters scope
   to a specific workspace's structure.
   - zgard/ws/ws.go: two new checks in PersistentPreRunE

Fix scroll stuck at top: stop using DISABLED state on transcript

all user edits while leaving see()/yview() fully functional.

perf(maglev): enable global promotion for functions with user calls

Previously, codegen-level global promotion was completely disabled when
any Call/Construct node existed anywhere in the function. This meant
benchmarks like prototype_chain, sieve_primes, and closure_counter
(which have calls in their setup code) lost all global promotion
benefits in their hot loops.

Now the codegen emits flush (sta_global) before and reload (lda_global)
after each user call node, keeping promoted register-file copies in
sync with GlobalEnv. The flush saves/restores live caller-saved
registers; the reload saves/restores all caller-saved registers
unconditionally since post-call liveness is not easily available.

For benchmarks with calls only in setup (prototype_chain, sieve_primes),
the flush/reload cost is paid once while the hot loop runs at full
speed with promoted globals in R14 slots.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: add version increment to tenant module UPDATE queries

The optimistic locking trigger (enforce_version_lock) rejects updates
where NEW.version <= OLD.version. When the UPDATE omits the version
column, PostgreSQL carries the old value forward, triggering the
conflict check. Add explicit version = version + 1 to both tenants
and user_tenant_associations UPDATEs in updateTenantModules().

* fix: allow negative amounts on credit notes in invoices CHECK constraint

The invoices_amounts_check constraint required all amounts >= 0,

commit.message:
Implement master proxy strategy with server-side Piped fallback

Backend Improvements:
- Add dual-layer extraction: play-dl → Piped API fallback
- Server-side Piped API call eliminates browser CORS issues
- Return source indicator (play-dl or piped) in response
- Better error logging for debugging extraction failures
- 15s timeout for more reliable extraction attempts

Frontend Improvements:
- Remove client-side Piped iframe fallback (now handled server-side)
- Add error state display with user-friendly messages
- Add source badge showing extraction method (Direct/Piped)
- Improved loading UI with "Trying multiple sources" message
- Cleaner error handling and user feedback

Why This Works:
✓ Zero CORS errors - browser only talks to Render backend
✓ Double-layer reliability - play-dl fails → Piped API kicks in
✓ Simplified frontend - single API endpoint, no fallback logic needed
✓ Better UX - users see which source is being used

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
